I ordered a pair of pants online and stopped by a Lululemon store to try them on. When they didn't work, I went to the register to return them and was told I couldn't return them until the next day because the purchase "hadn't run through their system yet." This was confusing, considering my credit card had already been charged. What made the situation worse was what came next: I was told that in order to process the return, the store would need to scan my California ID -- not just look at it, but scan it into their system. This is deeply concerning. In California, scanning a government ID is not legal unless the practice is clearly disclosed and the purpose is explicitly stated, typically for fraud prevention in limited situations. That was not the case here. No disclosure, no explanation, just "this is the only way we can do the return." There is also no logical reason for this request. My ID is not tied to my online purchase, it is not "in their system," and there is no matching function taking place. If the transaction truly hadn't posted yet, scanning my ID would not solve that issue -- so why collect that data at all? Between the system excuse that doesn't align with the credit card charge and the insistence on scanning my ID without transparency, this experience felt unnecessary, invasive, and poorly handled. I expect better from a brand that positions itself as premium and customer-centric. I left without completing the return and with serious concerns about Lululemon's in-store practices around customer data and privacy.

I’ve been wearing Lululemon for years now, and it’s hands down some of the best activewear out there—especially in South Florida’s brutal heat and humidity. The fabrics are breathable and lightweight, making it easy to stay comfortable even during the hottest days. Whether I’m hitting the gym, running outside, or just doing everyday things, Lululemon keeps me cool and looking good. The quality is second to none. Everything holds up over time, even with frequent wear, and the fit is always on point. Plus, I’ve had nothing but positive experiences with customer service—whether I’m in-store or shopping online, they’ve always been helpful and professional. My only issue is with the availability of medium and large sizes. Some of the popular pieces are often sold out or hard to find in my size, which can be a little frustrating. Still, Lululemon’s quality and comfort make it worth the hunt. I’ll definitely keep coming back for more. If you’re looking for activewear that performs well in the heat and lasts, Lululemon is the way to go!

They never used to do this and it’s a very bad practice. I’ve never had this happen with any other product I’ve ordered except Lulu. It’s like you’re trying to maximize sales at the expense of poor customer service. Either change the way you handle your orders and fix how you handle your inventory with online sales. I don’t like wasting my time and unless this is resolved you’ve lost a long time customer. I’m sure I’m not the only one that agrees with this poor practice. Get your service together and in order.

I have been a long-term customer of lululemon since around 2014. Back then, I was consistently satisfied with the products I received. Although they were more expensive, the quality and fit were phenomenal. However, I was let down multiple times by lululemon in the past 2 years. 1. The Textured Double-Knit Cotton Hoodie cost 128 CAD in 2021 and has increased to 158 CAD, now made with more plastic-like material. 2. The ABC pants changed their fit, and what was once the perfect fit for my athletic build is now baggy and fits more like pajamas. 3. The delivery is handled by a local network. Orders placed before 7:30am EST cannot be canceled because the only cancellation method is through the call center, which opens at 9:30 EST. I also submitted a change-of-delivery request, but Lululemon was unresponsive and did not confirm the address in time. 4. The Johnny Collar Polo (I can't recall the exact name) has an opening so tight that I have to literally squeeze my head into it. 5. They forgot to remove the security tag for me twice at two different physical stores, and the funny thing is I didn't even trigger the security alarm. I'm pleased to see other companies catching up and gaining market share from lululemon, and I believe this trend is completely justified from my perspective. I have no issue paying more as long as I receive equivalent value in return :)

On 8 December 2025, I purchased 3 items from this company. On 18 Dec 25, seeing nothing had shipped, I contacted the seller. DON'T go here. Every thursday at 6 or 7 I go here to shit. BUT I CAN'T. WANNA KNOW WHY? BECAUSE OSWALD IS ALREADY THERE, TAKING A SHIT. I’m here to leave a bad review like many others. I bought a $100 gift card, and when I first checked the balance, it showed the full $100. A few hours later, I checked again, and it said the card could not be found. It’s now being “looked at.” I called customer service right away. The first representative I spoke to was very nice and helpful. She said she could see that the $100 was still on my card and wasn’t sure why it wasn’t showing up on my end. Today, I called back to follow up, and I got no answers from the representative. She basically told me I was going to be out of luck — and then she hung up on me mid-sentence. Customer service literally hung up on me. It was absolutely horrendous. Lululemon seems to be operating an organized scam with their gift cards. There are thousands of people who have been stolen from. Go check Reddit — it’s full of stories about Lululemon gift card fraud and issues with their security team. I don’t understand how they’re getting away with this. They are stealing their customers’ money left and right, all while providing the worst customer service imaginable. My money was still showing on my card earlier, so I guess that’s one positive. I’m hoping to at least get a new card issued. I do not want them stealing my $100, and I will not let this go. You will hear from me every single day until I get a new card or my existing card is fixed — I promise you that. I also want to point out that I did not have an account, so my card wasn’t attached to anything. According to what I’ve read on Reddit, attaching your card to your account is when it gets stolen most often. This is your warning: do not buy Lululemon gift cards unless you want your money stolen. How does my card show $100 one moment, and then suddenly say it cannot be found? Because the money was taken. If I had read the reviews first, I would have never purchased this gift card. It was meant to be a gift, and now I can’t even give it. I’m shocked that a large company like this is getting away with what is basically organized theft — a pattern of corrupt activity that needs to be fully investigated by the FBI or whoever handles this kind of consumer fraud. They have stolen thousands of dollars from customers and then hang up on people who simply want answers. It’s like they don’t care at all because they’ve gotten away with it for so long. Karma will come. Please issue me a new card or fix my existing one immediately. Otherwise, you will be hearing from me every single day until I get my money back. I promise you, you will get sick of hearing from me — so let’s handle this ASAP.

I went to the Lululemon store at Ross Park to purchase outfits for a couple of women having surgery. I hoped to find outfits for them to feel special at such a difficult time. He gave me the in store gift card and I left with the store managers name. I reached out to Carey Enzsweiler and the conversations have been unprofessional. Ms. Enzsweilers response has been to tell me that she cannot find any proof that the tags were marked - they were not - and continues to say that she has to find more proof that I am telling the truth. If there is not any proof that the tags were marked, why is this an issue?

Customer service is rude and unhelpful. Their products are overpriced, and some look very worn after a short period of normal use. Overall, Lululemon feels more focused on fashion than quality, and the poor customer service made it a really disappointing shopping experience.

From Italy, I purchased a €250 gift card online as a present for my daughter, who is their customer and lives in London. When she went to the store, she was told she couldn’t use it because it had been purchased on the European website (something that was not specified at the time of purchase). I tried writing again to explain how impractical this solution was, but I never received another reply. They took the money, but I cannot use the gift card. Very unprofessional.

I bought a pair of pants at this store last week. When I brought them home, I took off the tags and put them on. Then I realized that they did not take off the security tag at the store. Look, mistakes happen, but you need to own up to them and give your customers what they paid for. Lululemon makes nice clothes, but this is just shady business practices.

Awful.. I got a pair of Align leggings 4 years ago and loved them - wore them seriously about 3 times a week for 4 years. I placed an order for 5 items which were the foundation of gifts. After 11 days, they had not arrived. Terrible! Rude customer service over a problem they caused!

The most horrible slow return process ever, they shit the item back to be destroyed, that takes up to 10 days, then they email saying you'll get a voucher. What is happening with the fashion at Lululemon it’s feel like the quality of the product has gone downhill and the price has gone up. I feel like there’s clowns in the office That lost inspiration. They need to get off their phones and stop scrolling and start creating.

Lululemon Customer Experience Review – Shockingly Poor for a Supposed Premium Brand I recently purchased a pair of Lululemon pants, and upon receiving them, I immediately discovered the snaps were faulty. These are not inexpensive pants—they are marketed and priced as premium apparel—so I expected premium service in return. Instead, the customer service agent refused to resolve the issue directly and immediately. Rather than offering an exchange or refund on the spot, I was directed to a website where I had to take photos, fill out forms, and essentially jump through hoops to prove the defect on a product I had just received. After doing all of that, I received an automated email informing me that someone would get back to me in 7–10 days—not to resolve it, but merely to review my claim. For a brand that claims to value quality and customer loyalty, this is completely unacceptable. This was a manufacturing defect, not customer damage, yet the burden was entirely placed on me. Delaying a resolution by more than a week shows a clear lack of accountability and care for the customer. As a result, I will not be making any future purchases from Lululemon. There are many competitors in the market offering similar or better quality—without subjecting their customers to an infuriating and dismissive claims process. At this rate, with these horrendous policies and zero sense of urgency or responsibility, I genuinely question how long Lululemon will be able to retain loyal customers or remain competitive in this industry. If this is their idea of customer service, I predict they won’t be in business much longer.
Extremely slow and lacks knowledge
ヨガ＆ピラティスのイベントに参加するため、ずっと欲しいと思っていたルルレモンのヨガウェアを買いました。欲しいと思っていたものの中には、オンライン限定のものがあったので、オンラインで利用しました。日本人にとっては高いと思いがちですが、世界中で愛されている理由がわかった気がします。今度はオフラインで利用したいです！

If you get a Lululemon bag, take care of it. The styles are always evolving and you may never see the design again. I just got a crossbody bowler bag. This is the second time this happens to me with Lululemon, and it’s beyond frustrating. I placed a large order and received most of it, but one item that was shipped separately never arrived at my door. I was home all day, I have camera footage, and the delivery photo they uploaded is not even my door. I contacted their customer service more than five times, and all I got were copy-and-paste responses like talking to a robot. They refuse to issue a refund, even though I provided clear proof that the leggings were never delivered. It’s shocking that a company of this size treats paying customers with such indifference. They make it impossible to get real help and act like losing your money is your problem. I’ve been a loyal customer for years, but after this, never again. Lululemon needs to learn what accountability and customer care mean.
Terrible return policy and extremely rude customer service! Customer service hung up on me without letting me finish my sentence. She was just not willing to help me at all .

I just ordered a vegan purse on Friday October 10, 2025 online. The next morning I got an email saying that it would be delivered on Monday October 13, 2025 which is Thanksgiving. I had it delivered to my place of business which is closed on holidays. Why would you ship something for delivery on a holiday weekend. When I contact customer service to change the shipping address I was told they may or may not be able to do it. What? How can you not change an address? Then the shipping company tried to deliver it to my work place which was closed. It is now in limbo and sent back to the shipping depot. I contact Lululemon customer service again and was told it was being returned to their warehouse for refund. I never asked for that. I wanted it delivered. When I said that to the agent I was informed you cannot change address unless it is FEDEX. Fedex was not an option for shipping. Then I was told to contact the shipping agent GoBolt. What is the point of the Lululemon customer service then? Why is it my responsibly to contact the shipper. I didn't ship it. The woman Amanda was not helpful in anyway. For my troubles, they said they would send me a 20 dollar gift card. My troubles is that I want the purse I ordered, I want Lululemon customer service to do their jobs instead I had to spend my 30 min lunch making sure GoBolt will reattempt a delivery. The cost of Lululemon should include better customer service. They have lost a customer. Both agent were dumb and didn't know how to do their jobs. Why bother hiring people for customer service, if they don't service any customers and pawn it off on the shipper.

Tried to return and they told me I couldn’t because I wore it once. I tried it just to see if it would fit and I couldn’t return it. I shopped here for years but not much after this.

I wanted to purchase a pair of Charge 3 shoes in size 9.5. They were sold out in my size online and the only store that had them was Willowbrook. I’ve been a long-time Lululemon customer, but after my recent experience, I will be rethinking any future purchases. Not only have I been disappointed with the declining quality of their products, but the customer service process has been frustrating, dismissive, and unhelpful. I reached out to Lululemon regarding a defective product - defective in the way that I wore the workout shirt twice and did not wash it and it had multiple holes. Instead of standing by their so-called “quality promise,” they dismissed my concern as “accidental damage.” Save this future line and deem everything as a "manufacturing issue". To make matters worse, they offered me a 25% off promo code as a consolation when I asked for a replacement or refund. While not ideal, I was willing to accept this, however, they failed to clearly state the expiration details in the last email they had sent. By the time I tried to use it, the code had already expired. When I followed up, I was met with nothing but canned responses and policies. They flat-out refused to reissue the code and spewed what seemed like generic AI-generated emails that did not always reveal true solutions that came close to exemplifying true quality customer service. Considering the amount of time I’ve wasted emailing back and forth, it’s unacceptable that they couldn’t provide a simple solution like reissuing the coupon they had already promised. An easy solution. This whole experience shows how little Lululemon values loyal customers. Their product quality is slipping, and their customer service team is rigid and unwilling to resolve issues fairly. A company that once prided itself on premium standards now feels more like a fast-fashion brand with premium prices. Save yourself the headache - there are plenty of other activewear brands that stand behind their products and treat their customers with respect. Unfortunately, Lululemon no longer seems to be one of them.

I have been a Lululemon online customer for years. Recently for some reason that customer service can't explain I was locked out of my online account. This is absolutely ridiculous as I did not need to show that when I opened my account. Also no other company ask for such private info. When I told them I was not comfortable doing this they said they could not unlock my account. To check out you need a security code on your credit card as you do in most cases, but this was not good enough for them. This is definitely not an ethical way to do business. I even tried to order my items online without an account and they still would not let me until I sent in my credit card statement. I will no longer be doing business with this company.

If I could give a zero star I would. Horrible Customer service. I had purchased $750 dollars of gift cards off air miles. The order went through but when I tried to use my gift cards, all the balances were zero. I contacted Lulu and they stated that the cards were used 4 days after I purchased them. I did not use these cards. I asked if they were used, where were they used and where was it sent? They then told me it is a privacy policy to not release information regarding the cards. They are my cards, I have a right to know where they were used! From there, they then stated that they actually cannot look up any transaction with said cards and that I will get no compensation. I truly believe that there was never a balance on these cards; or they re-use the numbers. Since purchased online, they assume zero responsibility. They told me to contact air miles. Air miles did their part correctly and paid the merchant. The issue is your cards have no balance and you refused to tell me where they were used, only that they were redeemed. They state that the cards are like cash and cannot be traced. I do not believe that for a second. Since they have zero transparency with these kind of purchases…. DO NOT BUY gift cards because if you have an issue, you are never getting that money back and they do not care. This was theft, plain and simple.

It did not get caught on anything or pulled, it simply popped open as I lifted the top onto the clothesline. As can happen with faulty knit fabric, it just zipped open in a straight line. Quite obviously, there was a flaw in the manufacture. I tried to start an online return, but they refuse to allow a return citing ‘wear and tear’. I have never worn the top. It would be funny if it wasn’t so wrong - and frustrating. Genuinely shocked by their customer service. I feel powerless to address this. worst customer service experience ever. i had two packages delivered incorrectly and contacted customer service for help. the educator i spoke to (via chat) basically told me to shop online next time and to call the cops instead of helping me resolve the issue. as if this made me want to shop online again? when i asked for someone else she rudely ended the chat while i was still talking. it wasnt until i called and spoke to someone else who told me that their system doesnt let them do anything about missing packages for 24 hours, which was not accurately relayed to me by the online educator (Kathleen). coming from working customer service before, this was sooooo incredibly rude and disrespectful behavior for something that was not my fault. their courier didn’t follow delivery instructions resulting in my packages being lost, which somehow equates to me being berated over text? they have the contract with the courier which is their responsibility. will neeverrrr shop lululemon again

If I could give no stars I would! I placed an online order for items I was buying for my granddaughter. The order was over $400! The receipt in the package showed all 5 items and the information on the website showed that all 5 supposedly were shipped in one package. I have made many calls regarding this matter. I was finally able to file a claim (you have to wait 24 hours from the delivery date) and even then you supposedly wait 2 or 3 days until they do an investigation and will offer the most "reasonable solution!" I am out $267 for which I received nothing. I asked to have this elevated and the customer service rep states that is not possible. I have only purchased in store previously. They forgot to take the security tag off the pant I bought from Willowbrook Mall store. I had to drive all the way back to the mall. The manager didn’t care about all hassle I had to go through. Very bad customer service in the store!!!

I ordered from Lululemon for over $400, trusting the brand to deliver quality — not just in the product, but in the experience. Lululemon used Veho as the delivery partner. The package was marked as “delivered”, but I never received it. The “proof of delivery” is literally a photo of a black screen — no visible location, no signature, no human confirmation. I reached out to Lululemon customer support asking for assistance — not even a refund, just to help me get a response from Veho or escalate the issue. They refused to help, saying the delivery was “confirmed.” But I have video surveillance from my building that proves no one showed up at the time Veho claimed delivery happened. I’m a single mother raising a baby. $400 is a significant amount for me. Lululemon’s response was dismissive and lacking all empathy — and no effort was made to investigate or protect me as a customer. Lululemon, you chose Veho. That makes you responsible. I expect: A formal investigation or dispute on my behalf, A refund or store credit, A review of your delivery partner’s behavior. Until then, I will continue sharing my story publicly — because this is not acceptable from a brand that charges premium prices.

I fell prey to all the cool advertising for this company, before checking reviews. BIG mistake. I used their sizing chart for their sliders and measured my son’s foot at 9.5 inches. Their sizing chart recommended an 8.5 in size. They recommended size 13 for me. My son is neurodivergent, and didn’t tell me during the first week of wearing them that it rubbed the top of his foot raw and gave him blisters under the arch. I wanted to like them. I ordered their blue and green slider. The comfort was ok, but for me my right foot slid forward over the tip. I didn’t wear them. I called and emailed my concerns and they arranged the return, which ended up a fiasco! Multiple phone calls and emails and it appears they don’t take notes bc I had to re-explain whats going on several times. It’s been about 3 weeks since all 3 pairs were sent back, which they confirmed they received. I finally received a store credit for one pair yesterday and sent an email asking them to confirm which pair that credit is for. I have not heard back yet. I don’t want a credit—their recommendations are faulty and their slider caused harm to my son’s foot. I’ve reached out to them and let them know I don’t want anything to do with their company and waiting to hear back.

BAD QUALITY PRODUCT NEVER AGAIN. One wash following "care" instructions = Completely destroyed hoodie. Customer service blamed me, refused to compensate. I bought the Everywhere Backpack (Black) from Lululemon in April 2024 and within two months the top strap started fraying. By August, it was already partially detached. I only carried a MacBook Air and a few daily items to and from my office—nothing heavy or rough. I continued using the bag carefully, hoping it wouldn’t get worse. I stopped using a bag a bit because I was not working. Then in April 2025, the strap completely tore off, making the bag unusable. I contacted Lululemon with photos clearly showing the issue started just two months after purchase. Their response? They claimed it was “wear and tear” and that I was outside the return window—despite photo evidence with dates and a clear timeline showing the damage began well within the one-year Quality Promise. It feels like they’re using the policy as a shield rather than standing by it in good faith. The evidence was solid, and they just didn’t want to take responsibility. For a company that positions itself as a premium brand, this was incredibly disappointing. The backpack cost nearly $80 and didn’t even last a few months under light use. I expected better quality—and definitely better customer support. Buyers beware: not everything Lululemon makes is built to last, and they may not stand behind their products even when they clearly fail early.
